one_time_description: Treaty Land Entitlement reconciliation receivable of $1,000,000 recorded; TLE revenue of $1,023,453 vs budget of $24,200
economic_notes: Nation operates marina, golf course, restaurant, accommodations, VLT, land leases (lot leases up to 69 year terms), property tax system, GST and sales tax revenue sharing. Development Corporation includes Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd. and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd. CMHC housing includes 20-unit rental project, 2-unit rental project, and 3-unit rental project.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Province is Manitoba. Four consolidated entities: Buffalo Point First Nation Housing Authority, Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd., Buffalo Point First Nation, and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd. Large deferred revenue balance of $13.5M mainly from lot leases (up to 69-year terms) and ISC deferred funding. Net debt position of $3.75M. Promissory note of $198,623 payable to Receiver General, unsecured and interest-free, past maturity date pending renewal. Contaminated sites exist but no liability recorded. TLE revenue spike ($1,023,453 vs $22,362 prior year) is notable. ISC deferred revenue increased significantly from $790,679 to $3,151,652. Capital lease obligations of $134,578 also included in liabilities. Annual debt service includes long-term debt repayment of $184,435 and capital lease repayment of $40,842.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
